title: periploca repens
intro: Periploca repens, also known as serpent wood or creeping periploca, is a woody vine native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Asia, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Periploca repens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Periploca repens is commonly prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional herbal remedies in the form of ointments and salves.
